reviews.review_text:
Great place to visit with hiking trails from the campground but I would never stay there again (this was not the first time ive stayed here & nothing's changed)
We were camping,  fire pit bbqs are old & unusable, showers are terrible with very little pressure and little to no hot water,  could do with more amenities and camp kitchens.
The shower in the unpowered sites were luke warm. Staff at the info centre were great, give them a pay rise. Way too many lights in the campground, are people scared of the dark?

Sehr schöner campground! Nettes Personal, alles sehr sauber.
Bei Rezeption kann man frühstücken, Kaffee holen, was trinken und einen Pool gibt's auch. Es gibt sogar einen kleinen Laden und man kann sogar tanken, wenn notwendig. Aber ist da mitten im nirgendwo natürlich alles ein bisschen teurer.
Außerdem ist direkt neben dem Gelände ein kleiner Hügel auf dem man sehr schön Sterne beobachten oder den Sonnenaufgang beobachten kann. Wandern kann man da auch sehr gut.
Würde auf jeden Fall wieder hinfahren!
Good size sites, quite a few had shade. One of the best stocked shops in have seen in an outback situation. Great prices.

Great location as trail heads all start here. Beautiful view of the rocks which was lovely especially at sunset and sunrise.
The 3 stars is for the amenities. 300m walk to the ablution block, unusable water pressure if more than one person is in a shower. Expect to have to run around in there to get wet by the tepid water. Then they were closed for cleaning at 8.30am, right when everyone is trying to get ready for the day. $70 a night for the privilege as a family of 5.

The store is well stocked, fuel available and check in staff very friendly.

Drive to the dump point.

We picked here as they have a lovely swimming pool but water temp was about 15 degrees when we were here so the kids didnt get in anyway.

Happy hour 3pm till 5pm at the bar 😊

We took our caravan, the park is huge. We love being in nature and here you are in the thick of it. Last night a quoll visited us at our sites, this doesn't happen everyday! Plenty of walks for to enjoy and tracks with some awesome 4WD tracks in the region.

We had a couple of nights at Wilpena Pound, staying in one of the one bedroom units at the resort. The location is great, right at the start of a number of great hiking trails and biking routes. There is also a very well-stocked shop on site as well as a good (but expensive) restaurant. Our room was bright and sunny with plenty of windows and the room was clean and comfortable. We were disappointed that there was no kitchenette at all – just a mini fridge and coffee maker. Some basic cutlery and plates/bowls plus a microwave would have been excellent. The AC worked well, but not many TV channels to choose from. The Wifi signal doesn’t extend to the units but you can access Wifi in the restaurant/bar/reception area. That being said, there is a decent cell signal there so easy to access the internet by cell phone. Overall we had a great stay and would recommend it to other travellers.
Stayed in camp grounds. They are updating facilities.  Need to update signage as most of it is unreadable.

Wir sind mit dem Camper dort gewesen. Dort gibt es sehr sehr nette Mitarbeiter, die uns geholfen haben. Unser Dank gilt ganz speziell Paul von der Haustechnik.
Der Pool hat sehr gutes Wasser und brennt nicht in den Augen.
Die Sanitären Anlagen sind für das Outback angemessen. Die Wege dorthin sind etwas weit. In der Campkitchen gibt es keinen Herd zum Kochen.
Es gibt kein Trinkwasser! Aber man bekommt kostenlos 10l Wasser pro Tag aus dem Shop.
Wunderschön sind die morgendlichen Besucher, auch nachts kommt mal jemand vorbei.

Some friends and I stayed here the 29th to 1st Feb2026, in one of the cabins and found it great.  The cabin was all that we needed and even when the fridge wasn’t getting as cold as we liked, one of the staff quickly found another fridge for us and it was all good.  We used the pool, whilst there and it was refreshing given the +40 degree heat.  The location of the resort was also great for walks/hikes and travelling to various other locations, viewing points etc.  On one night we used the restaurant and the food was excellent.  The only negative was the slight confusion regarding check-in/out, but quite minor in the scheme of things
Great to see the ranges

Great camp site, very well kept, clean amenities,  quiet and spacious (off season), great walks in the vicinity. There is a well-stocked general store. The resort has a restaurant which is very expensive. Hardly anything under $40,  but excellent food.
Great facilities!
Toilets are a bit in need of an upgrade, but a stunning location make up for it. Watch out for the emus, they are everywhere here.
Nice and quite. Walk trails well sign posted. 10 ltr drinking water free every day as all other water not drinkable. Shower and toilet blocks need major overhaul.

Absolute worst caravan park in over 70 I've stayed in on my lap around Australia.

Terrible design with all the facilities on the outside and it's huge so they can be a long way. Did 6kms one day, just doing my chores!
Customer service was terrible as many Australian campsites. Would rather argue why it's like that than listen to customers.
Dump site closed sign at the dump, about 1km away from the park. Not fun trundling a full cassette there and back. Not interested in my suggestion of posters in camp loos or on the drive. Would rather argue.
Didn't message when they said they would.
One guy didn't even stop working on his laptop whilst saying he couldn't answer my query about a local road because it wasn't his responsibility!
Would like to say avoid but it's the only one in the area so they have a monopoly and don't have to care about customers.

Great place to stay.

The shop is well stocked and I love that they give you free water. Also a good selection of souvenirs to purchase.

The amenities were plentiful and all very clean. I constantly saw the cleaners cleaning the toilet blocks every single morning. The toilets never ran out of soap or paper towels.

You get to choose your own campsite and the best part was that the walking track starts right here at the caravan park so when you return tired after a long hike it’s only a hop, skip and jump to your campsite. No driving required at all.

They are also safety conscious and send you an alert of there’s something to be concerned about - we got a wind warning on our last day there.

No drinking water here! I  was very unhappy that it was not made clear before we arrived. They will give you  some from the shop but it's far from ideal and it seems others were surprised too.
The amenities are not cleaned properly either. The walls of the shower cubicles have flaking paint and obviously haven't been wiped for months.  Paint doesn't flake like that in a short period.
Camp kitchens and BBQ areas weren't cleaned at all while we were there and were filthy.
The camping area does have lots of trees and is quite nice but the toilets can be far away as there are only two blocks and it is a large camp ground. Showers are good and hot.
Shop very basic. If you are going to Leigh creek go there. Huge supermarket.
Nice park and surrounds are spectacular. Showers were not always hot. Would have liked more child friendly walks available.
We had a powered site- 23 right up the top looking out on the trees- room for a camp fire- prefect location

It's been over 20yrs since I visited but it feels just like I remember. The buildings and amenities are newer and much better than they were. The staff were amazing, polite and helpful. I was visited by possums, quolls and a variety of birds at my camp-site. The scenery is incredible and there are some stunning drives and walks you can take in the area. Will be back very soon 💚
Great sites, quiet  lots of walking tracks, lots to see in the area well worth the stay and visit
Nice camp ground/caravan park/resort located in one of the most beautiful ranges on earth.
Amazing place to stay with our caravan.  What a destination too.  Loved the drives thru and around the National Psrk area.  Especially the Bunyeroo Gorge and Brachina Gorge roads.
Nice walk but disappointed l couldn't look inside.

Park setting is beautiful. Staff are friendly and helpful. However amenities  are dirty and rubbish in toilets and bins not emptied. Maintenance needs to happen as washing machines  leak water and laundry is along way from sites.

Beautiful area, good sites, store well stocked. However amenities were filthy and hadn't been cleaned the day we arrived. Mop was so dirty with red mud couldn't  be used and toilets had paper on floors everywhere. Showers had dirt all over the floor. Not sure if short staffed but a disappointment as there were alot of sites taken. We have travelled throughout SA and these were the dirtiest we have encountered. All other parks have been very clean and well maintained. A credit to the owners.
We were camping for a couple of nights. Really nice camp-site at a reasonable price and with very good amenities.
If you’re looking for a restful morning (especially if you have family or a big day ahead) I’d say go elsewhere - we woke to a chorus of motorbikes starting their day before first light.
Only camp ground in Wilpena pound. Powered sites are numbered, unpowered sites are a find your own. Can get very busy.

Be aware that there is no TV reception without
Starlink or Satellite in the caravan park. Telstra 4G available. Power available. Non potable water on tap and drinking water supplied free through the  store.

We looked at a few camping ground, glad that we came here. from SA up to North, I think this is the most beautiful camping ground - either a cabin or uncovered sites, it will offer you what you need.

The hiking trails nearby will satisfy all levels of hikers or natural lovers.
1: 6.4km steef up to Mt Ohlssen Bagge - 1000 meters top, a very challenging and satisfying one. We saw a gentleman who is 78 and many seniors climbing.
2: 6km round trip to Hills Homestead, a easy and nice walk,
3: 21.5km St Mary's Outside Trail - a day trip. we did part of it - starting from the camping ground- it was a beautiful trail.
4: Bridal Gap: 20km return, the first 8.5km is flat. a relatively easy walk.
5: there was also a trail to a waterfall.

Huge camping site, free for you to choose where u would like to camp - which I love about.

First night full moon, no milky way here. be aware of birthday party. I had to get out of my warm bed to request people to lower their voice. plenty of spaces for family, children play and bike riding.

The only area that needs improvement is the toilet - it needs an upgrade and also regular cleaning. if you have a toilet fetish, go to Rosleys. If you love natural, definitely Wilpena Pound.

Though the reception does not explain properly where to go for hiking, and where the shuttle bus is, you have to ask and prepare for your day outings.

Either seasoned traveller or first timer, you will not be disappointed.
What a relaxing place. We stayed 10 days, did some gorge drives, hiked to Hill settlement, read a book or 2 and just chilled.
Powered campsite was great, room to spread out. Showers were below average. Bring extra drinking water and plenty of firewood.
Amenities were good was cold.

We loved the National Park feel in amongst the trees and so much wildlife.  Large powered site with fireplace.  Easy to access walks and central for Brachina Gorge, Razorback lookout, Moralana scenic drives and multiple lookouts.  Laundry facilities were vastly inadequate for amount of people camping/ caravanning.  Glad we were there in a quieter period. Drinking water was provided by park free.
Wilpena Pound camping area is fantastic, and we arrived in drizzling rain and it was still magical. You get a 10l water container to boot

Been to Flinders before, but, first time in the caravan park. Our site was great, beautiful views of the range and all the surrounding bush. Plenty of space on site, amenities were good and clean. The walk to Hills Homestead was just amazing and the pioneers were incredible, also , a must is a look and walk around old Wilpena homestead definitely worth the effort.
We had a caravan site. Nice and shady, fairly level and modern clean amenities but the dearest site we have paid for, 2 nights for $128, and that's with our NRMA discount.
Wilpena pound dis a fantastic spot to spend a couple of days.

This place is not a Resort. It’s a tired old motel complex that is very over priced. Stayed 2 nights in Aroona Room. The rooms are very basic, clean, the bed comfortable & the shower good ( a bonus after trekking). The staff are polite and helpful. The walking experiences, scenery etc are sensational. On a positive note I recommend you visit the Wilpena Pound Historic Homestead, it is very interesting.

We stayed in the Brachina room number 8 and it was very clean and modern on the inside and we loved it.  Good hot water for showers and comfy bed.  Staff were all friendly and really helpful.  We had dinner in the restaurant and were very happy with our well cooked steaks.  Breakfast the next morning was also great, we had continental for $20pp.  We booked a 1 hour flight before we came and were very pleased to have a beautiful sunny day.
Excellent

The fact this is advertised as a resort is false advertising it is a grotty old motel. It is daylight robbery charging $300 per night for a room. We have stayed at many remote resorts around Australia and this is by far the worst. Ironically we even booked through “luxury escapes”. Unfortunately they can get away with charging this because are no other options in the area. |The Flinders ranges is beautiful and worth a visit but stay elsewhere if you can or consider a campervan if you must come here. ||From the moment we entered our Aroona room we were shocked. It is in desperate need of a renovation but even before that some urgent changes are required. The bed was the hardest we have ever slept on. The bathroom bin was rusted and disgusting. There is no separate hand soap and it had to be moved between the shower and sink constantly. The shower recess had nowhere to put the shampoo bottles which meant they had to go on the stained floor of the shower that you felt you needed to wear thongs as though you were in the communal caravan park bathroom. Our curtain didn’t close properly near at the end that faces the bed. We also appeared to be missing a sheer curtain compared to other rooms which meant we had to have the block out curtains constantly closed for privacy. The rooms are advertised as being serviced daily but on our first day we returned late to find it had not been serviced and we had to ask bar staff for new towels and water as reception had closed. |On the second day we had to ask a staff member to service our room as by the end of the day it was also not going to be serviced again. ||We had breakfast included in our booking and despite us attending as it opened at 7am it was cold and limited. ||The advertised camp kitchen for use by all resort guests only has bbq, fridge and microwave it does not have a stove for cooking. The IGA also isn’t regularly stocked so ensure you bring any fresh provisions with you. ||The staff were mostly helpful and friendly and I do not place blame on them.  This needs to be addressed by Discovery parks management as a whole. Staff can only work with what they are given.
Great place for a camping holiday, either in tents, an RV or renting a room.

We are two nights into a three-night stay and we’ve been really happy with the Wilpena Pound Resort. The resort is now with Discovery and we haven’t seen any evidence of the poor service of earlier reviews. |We’re in a cabin, which has been really nice. It has a kitchenette, dining table and outdoor table and chairs, double bed and bunk bed for the kids plus a neat ensuite bathroom. All very comfortable and clean. The rooms even get serviced each day, which is a bonus. |We’ve eaten at the restaurant for lunch and dinner and both times it’s been really nice. The menus for both meals feel like a bit of effort has been put in to make the options interesting, tasty and varied. It’s not just steak and chips or burger and chips, although you can get that if that’s what you feel like. |We self-catered for breakfast and would do so again. The kitchenette is well stocked and it’s a good way to save money because the buffet breakfast is $30 for adults and $20 for kids. |The reception staff have been polite, helpful and knowledgeable. We also did a tour this morning, which was really interesting and the guide was very enthusiastic and friendly. |All in all, we’ve had a really great stay. I admit I was kind of worried when I saw some of the negative reviews but our experience has been nothing like that. Seems like the new management have given the resort an overhaul and lifted standards. Location is beautiful as well. Highly recommend.
Expensive for camping sites. Not too clean shower

Very disappointed with the state of the facilities at both the resort and caravan park
Badly in need of a cleanup or refurbishing
I was last here 10 yrs ago & it was well kept
Times have changed & such a shame
Also no walks under 2 miles No good for us oldies Used to be shorter walks & they were free Now $10 per person to walk on any trail in the park. All loose & facilities dirty !

A flat caravan site… for starters, that is a make or break for us! The sites were large too!
An IGA that is well stocked.
The amenities were very clean.
And quiet at nighttime. I even took some star photos, a bit out of focus, but I tried.
Close to all things Flinders Ranges.
Set amongst the most beautiful place.
Certainly ticked all our boxes.
Great location, not many communal caravan park facilities other than the resort and pool. Did I mention the great location?
Great park. Good facilities. Plenty to see, and do, or not, whatever floats your boat. Well set out. Not badly priced. Friendly reception. IGA. Restaurant. Pool. To name a few.

Wat een plek! We zouden 2 nachten blijven maar hebben nog een nacht extra geboekt. Het personeel is heel erg vriendelijk en leuk om een
praatje te maken over de verschillende wandelingen. Wij deden St Mary Peak en Mount Ohlssen. Super! Vanaf de camping zijn alle wandelingen aangegeven. We hadden geluk dat het park heel rustig is op dit moment. Bush camping met alle gemakken voorzien. Zelfs een zwembad voor een frisse duik na een verhitte wandeling.
Camping 3 nights unpowered, good shoulder season prices, shop there is quite cheap, found really cheap ginger beer there, clean facilities, nearby swimming hmm so refreshing

Camped in an unpowered site between Xmas and New Year. Had free choice of some excellent level and shady sites. Excellent facilities and access to the pool (not heated, so quite fresh). Bar restaurant serves good food but doesn’t open until 6 pm. The mini supermarket has many things but no meat during the off season.
Nice setting and great place to base yourself from. Amenities are good and it has services such as a shop and petrol/diesel available.

We stayed on an unpowered site which are mostly unser trees and have some shades. The amenities were clean. Small Shop on site, pool is quite small but enough for a afternoon swim. The Restaurant ist nice and has some good food. Easy to drive there, even with a 2WD as all way is gravel road. Good WIFI but just at the reception and at the restaurant, not in the camping itself.
Incredible nice employees in a super nice place ! Recommend 10000% keep rocking

Great location to spend a few days in nature. Camping facilities are everything you need after a hard day's hike, and yes, if you're hiking, it's going to be a hard day. The staff were all helpful and very friendly.
Fully self-contained van , this time of the year ample space ,October. $40 UMM bit rich , but no one makes u stay .

Seems more focused on there over priced resort.
Park map was outdated & not easy to navigated.
Facilities could be better up kept, especially for the amount of money asking.
Meals over priced & left looking not full.
Recommend staying else where & just day trip in, sorry & sad. We expected more.
Beautiful park, large sites. Bathroom amenities have been renovated!! Amazing, and the wildlife in the park we rr sharing our time with is calming!  Love it here.

Can't comment on the rooms as we stayed in the powered section of the Caravan Park.  Well laid out.  Clean amenities.   A short walk to the bar/bistro/pool/shop.  Decent phone reception (4g).  Plenty of bins for your rubbish.  Friendly, helpful staff.  We had site 44 which was huge and easy to reverse into with a 21ft van.

As far as outdoor camping sites go, this was a very well fitted out park and had all the amenities you’d need when camping.

There are warm showers and flushing toilets, which are cleaned regularly by the staff. It only gets gross because other people are lazy, so be courteous and clean up after yourselves. There are a few toilet blocks on site (pictured) and look relatively recently renovated.

There is also a laundry room and outdoor line to hang your clothes, but you’ll need to bring your own laundry detergent. (Pictured)

You’ll also see in the pictures that for the unpowered sites (where I camped) it’s a bit of a free for all, you can just find a nice patch of ground that you wanna set up, there’s no designated sites. There are spots with plenty of trees if you want to hang up a washing line or a hammock.

Also in fire season, you can just set up a fire as long as it’s 4m away from any vegetation, there are plenty of rocks around so you just make a little barrier with them, alternatively there are fire pits (pictured) with a fitted metal grill as well.

Not only are the amenities very good, but obviously the views and the hikes nearby make it even more worthwhile. We came here in early October, so there were lots of beautiful wild flowers all over the hikes and tracks, it also wasn’t too hot during the day but did get a bit cold at night (10 degrees).

You’ll also see lots of wild life just walking around the park. Got woken up by squawking birds and kangaroos jumping around our tent. We also saw blue tongue lizards, echidnas, and emus. Wildlife rangers mentioned their animal conservation program is going very well!

Wonderful van park in amongst the trees with native wildlife wandering around the area
Unfortunately the reticulated water is non-potable but the park provides 10 litre packs of spring water on request
Great place

Good location and helpful staff
But didn’t have a nice hot shower
Walks are good but the park needs to be tidied up

A really great place to stay and watch the world go by. stayed in non powered site with easy access to toilets and showers.not the first time we have stayed here and definitely won't be the last.WOULD highly recommended the trip there,if you're into hiking all the better or just sit back

Superb location for hiking in and around Wilpena, however the maintenance and cleanliness of the facilities leaves a lot to be desired. Tissues, dirty flannels and other rubbish left in the same space in the bathrooms for the duration of our stay, lots of limescale build up and brown staining around sinks/showers and huge fat collection tubs under the bbq overflowing. See pictures. The staff in visitor information were functional but unfortunately weren’t enthusiastic to share any recommendations or advice.
Great location with wildlife at your doorstep. Plenty to do.

Felt sorry for office staff as caravaners often don't go to correct bays.  Perhaps numbers need to be on each with numbers also showing on water & power. This would help office staff. Even a boom gate as I am sure some sneak in & pay nothing. That is just my opinion.

Beautiful bit of nature but not really my thing but it might be yours and that's cool. I'm not huge on caravan parks I guess.
To me everything felt overly managed and overly woke. Treated pine barriers, cramped allotments, signs on literally everything, wired off areas, fancy villas, stick to the paths, no wood collection.  I get why you need it at an industrial sized park but it's just too developed, it's just not for me.
That and for the price the toilets and showers could've been cleaned at least once a day and stocked with toilet paper with more than 2 out of 4 working.
Great stop
Great place to stay and see the Flinders Ranges
Clean amenities but still needs a few essentials, loved staying here.

Stayed here on a powered site. We wanted unpowered but the photos on their website only showed tents on unpowered sites so we were concerned we might not fit (yes, we could of called to clarify but who talks on the phone these days!?) There were some unpowered sites our 23 foot van would of fit on.
The caravan park is surrounded by trees. It's such a lovely setting. The roads are windy and trees hang low but we managed in our off-road van which is quite tall.
Starlink had issues because of the amount of trees about.
Bathrooms seemed brand new as they were so clean and well maintained.
My only negative is the lack of signing for where caravans go to check in. Signs all point to the resort check-in, I couldn't see any signs on caravan check-in.  FYI - you check in at the information/national parks desk.
Dirty, expensive and commercialized. Laundry and bathrooms never clean, hikes are 7km away so nothing you can do quickly
Beautifully placed amongst the trees and bushland a stones throw from the resort. The facilities are some of the cleanest I have come across. Will definitely be back.

Stayed here for 2 nights after staying 2 nights in the northern end of the park in the national park campgrounds. This is a great location to explore the walks around Wilpena pound but is quite a drive from the other attractions in the park and the road can be quite rough if driving back and forth. We paid $25 per night in the off season which was fantastic value given it was the same price for a bush camp in the park. Tons of space, good showers and laundry services etc. The swimming pool at the resort is well worth a visit too
We love camping here. Nice and piece full beautiful wild life. And has a pool. Toilet blocks are very clean and tidy.

Magnifique camping dans les flinder range , au milieu des bois, grands emplacements powered et un powered,  sanitaires propres, buanderie (4$ le lavage et 4$ le sechage) . Point d'informations et carburant à l'entrée du camping .
Excellent rapport qualité prix.  Nous avons payé 19$ pour une unpowered place .
Départ de randonnées à partir de ce camping selon la saison. Nous n'avons pas pu les faire pour cause  de canicule ( 40 °) .
Gros coup de cœur pour cet endroit.
Très beau camping dans le parc naturel avec des emplacements ombragés, très calme et proche de sentiers de randonnée

Loved the place, first visit with my family and friends. Itwould have been better if there were a shared common kitchen just like the common bathroom. The grocery shop was poverty stricken. Will return sometime again.

Cant comment on the rooms as we stayed at the caravan park absolutely stunning campground wildlife everywhere!! Check in was above and beyond helpful super informative gave us maps and told us where to go and how to get around. Beautiful bushwalks and hikes for all abilities (read the signs and maps to find a walk suitable) many walks start at the campgrounds. Only letdown was the amenities block the toilets and showers while stunningly renovated haven’t been cleaned the whole week we have been here but this could be because it’s the holiday season and they are running skeleton staff hopefully the rest of the time they are better maintained (and this we could ignore it’s not like you hang out in bathrooms all day). We have loved our stay here everything you need at the IGA fuel etc honestly been a favourite place we have stayed. Staff service with a smile and chat. Many scenic drives nearby we’d recommend a 4WD vehicle. It’s been great!!!
For a caravan park inside the national park.  Fantastic .
Beautiful place with spectacular views and bush walks.
Powered site. Quiet despite the place being quite busy.

Arrived at Visitor info centre. Paid our camp fee $35. Couple travelling with us paid $45 for an unpowered site. Questioned the difference told our friends had to pay SA Park entrance fee as well as campground fee. We used the campground as an overnight stop . Did not even walk to view the Pound. Seems a bit excessive especially when SA National Park site clearly states Park entrance fees are included in camping fees. Apart from that - we were not told about nonpotable water or the free water available at the shop. Toilet facilities could do with a good scrub. Soap not replenished. Paper and plastic rubbish along the fence near where we camped. Yes we picked it up. Will not stay again.

We spend one night on the unpowered site. We got 10l water as the tap water isn't drinkable.
We paid $34 for our car with tent, 2 adults.
We were happy about the long table in the campingkitchen. You have do bring everything on your own, there is just the sink and a BBQ.
Showers were hot!
Perfectly located caravan park and resort to be central to so many activities and sites of the Flinders Ranges
Great place to visit and stay!
